 {"id":"brenner_bot-719","title":"Task: Build search index generation script","description":"Create script to generate search index at build time.

## Purpose

Build-time index generation ensures:
- Fast search (no runtime parsing)
- Consistent index across environments
- Index can be cached/CDN'd

## Requirements

### Input
- All corpus documents (7 files)
- Parse each by type (transcript, distillation, quote-bank, metaprompt)

### Processing
- Extract searchable content
- Split into appropriate chunks (sections, paragraphs)
- Preserve metadata: docId, title, section, anchor

### Output
- Search index file(s) in format required by chosen library
- Place in public/ or generated/ directory
- JSON format for easy inspection/debugging

## Implementation

### Script Location
`apps/web/scripts/build-search-index.ts`

### Integration Points
- Add to build process: `prebuild` script in package.json
- Ensure index regenerates when corpus changes
- Consider watching corpus files in dev mode

### Document Processing

Each document type needs specific handling:
- **Transcript**: 236 numbered sections
- **Distillations**: Sections with headers
- **Quote Bank**: Individual quotes
- **Metaprompts**: Full document or sections

### Index Entry Schema
```typescript
interface SearchEntry {
  id: string;           // unique identifier
  docId: string;        // document ID for linking
  docTitle: string;     // document title for display
  sectionTitle?: string; // section header if applicable
  content: string;      // searchable text
  anchor?: string;      // URL anchor for deep linking
  type: 'transcript' | 'distillation' | 'quote-bank' | 'metaprompt';
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Script generates valid index
- [ ] All 7 documents indexed
- [ ] Index includes section-level granularity
- [ ] Anchors enable deep linking
- [ ] Script runs as part of build
- [ ] Index size documented","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:29:20.035530266-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:06:14.416930481-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:06:14.416930481-05:00","close_reason":"scripts/build-search-index.ts is complete - generates MiniSearch index at build time for all 7 corpus documents with section-level granularity and anchors","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-719","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-gnj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:30:22.401126205-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-719","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5i2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:30:22.463868729-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-751","title":"CLI: ignore invalid implicit config file","description":"## Problem
`brenner.ts` loads a default config file path (`~/.config/brenner/config.json` or `%APPDATA%\brenner\config.json`) even when the user did not explicitly opt in. If that file exists but is malformed JSON (or has wrong shape), the CLI hard-fails.

## Why this is bad
From a first-principles UX/reliability standpoint:
- The *default* behavior should be resilient: a stray/half-written config file should not brick the CLI.
- Explicit config (`--config` or `BRENNER_CONFIG_PATH`) should remain strict (fail fast) to avoid silently ignoring operator intent.

## Root cause
`loadBrennerConfig()` parses config unconditionally when the default path exists; parse errors throw.

## Fix
- If config path is **implicit** (default) and parsing fails, **fail-soft**: ignore the config and continue with env + defaults.
- If config path is **explicit** (`--config` or `BRENNER_CONFIG_PATH`), keep **fail-fast** behavior.
- Optionally emit a one-line warning to stderr when ignoring an implicit config (must not spam normal output).

## Acceptance
- `--config <path>` with invalid JSON => exit 1 with a clear parse error.
- Default path exists but invalid JSON => CLI still works (exit codes/outputs unchanged for normal commands).
- Unit tests cover the explicit/implicit behavior boundary.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T06:30:32.06869927-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T06:51:12.122426712-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T06:51:12.122426712-05:00","close_reason":"Fail-soft on invalid implicit config; fail-fast on explicit config; add bun tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-751","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.1.2","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T06:30:32.073465143-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-77nk","title":"Task: Add interactive mode to "brenner test execute"","description":"# Goal
Add an interactive prompt flow for `brenner test execute TEST_ID` so operators can record executions without manually specifying every flag.

This is a UX layer on top of the existing non-interactive (flag-driven) implementation.

---

# Background / Why
`brenner test execute` is the bridge between an executed discriminative test and hypothesis state updates (confirm/refute). In practice, the operator often has the observation in hand but doesn’t want to translate it into multiple CLI flags.

We already have:
- test records + execution recording (stored under `.research/tests/`)
- transition suggestions (kill/validate)
- optional auto-apply (`--apply`)

Missing: a first-class, low-friction operator UX for entering those fields interactively.

---

# Proposed UX (interactive mode)
When invoked as:

```bash
brenner test execute T-...
```

Prompt for:
1) Observed result/observation (free text)
2) Potency check: pass/fail (required)
3) Confidence: high/medium/low/speculative (default: medium)
4) Optional notes
5) (Optional / v0) “Apply suggested transitions?” (yes/no)
   - If yes: apply all or review one-by-one

Non-goal: running experiments. This command only records and binds results.

---

# CLI surface decisions
- Keep the existing flag-driven path working exactly as-is.
- Add `--interactive` to force prompts even if some flags are present.
- Add `--no-interactive` (or equivalent) to fail fast if required flags are missing (useful for scripts/CI).
- Ensure `--json` works in both modes (final output emitted as one JSON object).

---

# Implementation constraints
- Bun-only; no vendor AI APIs.
- Avoid adding heavy prompt dependencies if possible; prefer `node:readline`/stdin-based prompting.
- Ensure prompts do not trigger in non-TTY contexts unless explicitly requested (avoid hanging pipelines).

---

# Testing
Add CLI tests that drive stdin for interactive prompts. Approach:
- Extend the `runCli(...)` helper in `brenner.test.ts` to optionally write to stdin.
- Create a minimal temp project with a ready test + active hypotheses.
- Run `brenner test execute T-... --json` with scripted stdin input and assert:
  - execution recorded
  - suggestions returned
  - (if apply chosen) hypothesis states updated

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `brenner test execute TEST_ID` prompts for required execution fields when run interactively.
- [ ] Non-interactive usage via flags continues to work unchanged.
- [ ] Non-TTY runs do not hang (either require flags or require `--interactive`).
- [ ] Tests cover at least one interactive happy path (stdin-driven).","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"NavyMountain","created_at":"2025-12-31T03:54:37.42559785-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-06T02:18:31.991323459-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-77nk","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-gson","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-31T03:54:37.450934929-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-77t2","title":"Task: Test Data Fixtures Library","description":"# Goal
Create a comprehensive test data fixtures library for consistent, realistic test data.

# Fixture Categories

## 1. Document Fixtures
Location: `apps/web/src/__fixtures__/documents/`

```typescript
// distillation.fixture.ts
export const distillationFixture = {
  id: 'test-distillation',
  type: 'distillation',
  title: 'Test Distillation Document',
  sections: [
    {
      number: 1,
      text: 'This is the first section with some Brenner concepts.',
      anchors: ['§1'],
    },
    {
      number: 103,
      text: 'You\'ve forgotten there\'s a third alternative. Both could be wrong.',
      anchors: ['§103'],
      jargonTerms: ['third alternative'],
    },
    // ... more sections
  ],
};

// transcript.fixture.ts
export const transcriptFixture = {
  id: 'test-transcript',
  type: 'transcript',
  title: 'Test Transcript',
  paragraphs: [
    { number: 1, text: 'First paragraph of transcript.' },
    // ... more paragraphs
  ],
};

// quotebank.fixture.ts
export const quoteBankFixture = {
  quotes: [
    {
      id: 'q1',
      text: 'It is good to be ignorant about a new field.',
      section: '§230',
      tags: ['productive-ignorance'],
    },
    // ... more quotes
  ],
};
```

## 2. Session Fixtures
Location: `apps/web/src/__fixtures__/sessions/`

```typescript
// session.fixture.ts
export const activeSessionFixture = {
  id: 'test-session-active',
  status: 'active',
  research_question: 'Does role-separated prompting improve artifact quality?',
  excerpts: ['§103', '§105', '§230'],
  created_at: '2025-12-30T12:00:00Z',
  participants: [
    { agent: 'BlackCastle', model: 'opus-4.5' },
    { agent: 'PurpleHill', model: 'opus-4.5' },
  ],
  contributions: [],
};

export const completedSessionFixture = {
  id: 'test-session-complete',
  status: 'completed',
  // ... includes full artifact
};

export const errorSessionFixture = {
  id: 'test-session-error',
  status: 'error',
  error_message: 'Agent Mail connection failed',
};
```

## 3. User Fixtures
Location: `apps/web/src/__fixtures__/users/`

```typescript
export const authenticatedUserFixture = {
  id: 'test-user',
  email: 'test@example.com',
  name: 'Test User',
  role: 'researcher',
};

export const adminUserFixture = {
  id: 'admin-user',
  email: 'admin@example.com',
  name: 'Admin User',
  role: 'admin',
};
```

## 4. API Response Fixtures
Location: `apps/web/src/__fixtures__/api/`

```typescript
// agent-mail.fixture.ts
export const agentMailInboxFixture = {
  result: [
    {
      id: 1,
      subject: 'Test message',
      from: 'TestAgent',
      to: ['CurrentAgent'],
      body_md: '## Test\n\nThis is a test message.',
      importance: 'normal',
      created_ts: '2025-12-30T12:00:00Z',
    },
  ],
};

export const agentMailErrorFixture = {
  error: {
    code: -32000,
    message: 'Agent not found',
  },
};
```

## 5. Error Response Fixtures
```typescript
export const error404Fixture = {
  status: 404,
  error: 'Not Found',
  message: 'The requested resource was not found.',
};

export const error500Fixture = {
  status: 500,
  error: 'Internal Server Error',
  message: 'An unexpected error occurred.',
};
```

# Fixture Factory Functions
```typescript
// factories.ts
export function createSessionFixture(overrides: Partial<Session> = {}): Session {
  return {
    id: `session-${Date.now()}`,
    status: 'pending',
    research_question: 'Default research question',
    excerpts: [],
    created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
    ...overrides,
  };
}

export function createDocumentFixture(type: DocType, overrides = {}): Document {
  // ... factory implementation
}
```

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Document fixtures for all document types
- [ ] Session fixtures for all states
- [ ] User fixtures with different roles
- [ ] API response fixtures (success and error)
- [ ] Factory functions for dynamic fixture creation
- [ ] TypeScript types for all fixtures
- [ ] Documentation for fixture usage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:54:32.06709461-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T23:04:59.953396341-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T23:04:59.953396341-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive test fixtures library with documents, sessions, users, API responses, and factory functions. 55 tests all passing.","labels":["testing infrastructure fixtures"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-77t2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-4ep3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:37:11.886820429-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-7b6","title":"Add /corpus index page (doc listing + filters)","description":"# Task

Create `/corpus` index page that lists corpus documents with search + filtering **and** respects the content policy.

Key constraint:
- In **public mode**, only show allowlisted docs (see `brenner_bot-5so.7.6.3`).
- For restricted docs (e.g., full transcripts), either hide them or show a locked stub that requires lab-mode auth.

# Documents / categories

- Transcripts (restricted by default)
- Quote bank
- Metaprompts
- Distillations
- Model response batches

# Page layout

- Search bar at top
- Filter chips (All, Transcripts, Distillations, Metaprompts, Responses)
- Document cards in responsive grid

Document card fields:
- document title
- type badge
- brief description/excerpt
- word count / estimated read time
- access state (public vs locked)

# Implementation notes

- Prefer a curated doc registry (avoid arbitrary filesystem browsing).
- Ensure doc routing cannot be used to read arbitrary files outside the allowlist.

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Public mode lists only allowlisted docs (or locked stubs) and never serves restricted content.
- [ ] Search filters results.
- [ ] Type filters work.
- [ ] Links navigate to document viewer for allowed docs.
- [ ] Locked docs show a clear "how to unlock" UX.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-q4u9","title":"TASK: Define Data Migration Strategy for Schema Changes","description":"
## Evolving the Data Model Without Losing User Data

As the Brenner Loop evolves, the data schema will change:
- Adding new fields to HypothesisCard
- Restructuring EvidenceEntry
- New session metadata

Users have existing sessions in LocalStorage. We can't break them.

### Migration Architecture

\`\`\`typescript
// Version marker in all data
interface SessionData {
  _version: number;  // Schema version
  // ... rest of session
}

// Migration registry
const MIGRATIONS: Record<number, Migration> = {
  2: migrateV1ToV2,
  3: migrateV2ToV3,
  // etc.
};

const CURRENT_VERSION = 3;

// Migration function type
type Migration = (data: unknown) => unknown;
\`\`\`

### Migration Flow

\`\`\`
Load session from storage
         │
         ▼
Check _version field
         │
         ├── If current → Use directly
         │
         └── If older → Run migrations
                │
                ▼
         Run each migration in sequence:
         v1 → v2 → v3 → current
                │
                ▼
         Save migrated data back
                │
                ▼
         Continue with session
\`\`\`

### Example Migrations

\`\`\`typescript
// V1 → V2: Add confidence history
function migrateV1ToV2(data: SessionV1): SessionV2 {
  return {
    ...data,
    _version: 2,
    hypotheses: data.hypotheses.map(h => ({
      ...h,
      confidenceHistory: [{ 
        confidence: h.confidence, 
        timestamp: data.createdAt 
      }]
    }))
  };
}

// V2 → V3: Add prediction lock timestamp
function migrateV2ToV3(data: SessionV2): SessionV3 {
  return {
    ...data,
    _version: 3,
    hypotheses: data.hypotheses.map(h => ({
      ...h,
      predictions: h.predictions.map(p => ({
        ...p,
        lockedAt: p.locked ? data.updatedAt : null
      }))
    }))
  };
}
\`\`\`

### Migration Safety

1. **Backup before migration**:
\`\`\`typescript
async function safeLoadSession(id: string): Promise<Session> {
  const raw = localStorage.getItem(\`session:\${id}\`);
  const data = JSON.parse(raw);
  
  if (data._version < CURRENT_VERSION) {
    // Backup original
    localStorage.setItem(\`session:\${id}:backup:\${data._version}\`, raw);
    
    // Migrate
    const migrated = runMigrations(data);
    localStorage.setItem(\`session:\${id}\`, JSON.stringify(migrated));
    
    return migrated;
  }
  
  return data;
}
\`\`\`

2. **Rollback capability**:
\`\`\`typescript
function rollbackMigration(sessionId: string, toVersion: number): boolean {
  const backupKey = \`session:\${sessionId}:backup:\${toVersion}\`;
  const backup = localStorage.getItem(backupKey);
  
  if (backup) {
    localStorage.setItem(\`session:\${sessionId}\`, backup);
    return true;
  }
  return false;
}
\`\`\`

3. **Version compatibility check**:
\`\`\`typescript
function isVersionSupported(version: number): boolean {
  return version >= MIN_SUPPORTED_VERSION && version <= CURRENT_VERSION;
}
\`\`\`

### Testing Migrations

\`\`\`typescript
describe('Migrations', () => {
  test('V1 → V2 adds confidence history', () => {
    const v1Data = loadFixture('session-v1.json');
    const v2Data = migrateV1ToV2(v1Data);
    
    expect(v2Data._version).toBe(2);
    expect(v2Data.hypotheses[0].confidenceHistory).toBeDefined();
    expect(v2Data.hypotheses[0].confidenceHistory.length).toBe(1);
  });
  
  test('Full migration path V1 → current', () => {
    const v1Data = loadFixture('session-v1.json');
    const currentData = runMigrations(v1Data);
    
    expect(currentData._version).toBe(CURRENT_VERSION);
    expect(validateSession(currentData)).toBe(true);
  });
});
\`\`\`

### File Locations
\`\`\`
apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/index.ts
apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/v1-to-v2.ts
apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/v2-to-v3.ts
apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/migrations/__tests__/
\`\`\`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Version field in all persisted data
- [ ] Migration functions for each version bump
- [ ] Backup before migration
- [ ] Rollback capability
- [ ] Migration tests with fixtures
- [ ] Handles corrupted data gracefully

### Depends On
- 1v26.1 (Session Data Model)
- 1v26.2 (LocalStorage Persistence)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-qjzw","title":"Task: Add Scorecard CLI Commands","description":"# Task: Add Scorecard CLI Commands

## Objective
CLI commands for scoring sessions, viewing feedback, and comparing scores.

## Implementation Details

### Commands

#### `brenner score <session-id>`
```bash
brenner score RS-20251230

# Output:
Session Score: RS-20251230
```

```bash
  Suggestion: For each test, ensure hypotheses make DIFFERENT
```

 {"id":"brenner_bot-qka","title":"Feature: Mobile Reading Experience","description":"Optimize the experience of reading long-form content on mobile devices.

## Rationale

The primary use case for BrennerBot on mobile is READING — users reviewing transcripts, distillations, or quotes. The transcript alone is 40k+ words. This needs to feel effortless.

## User Scenarios

1. Reading through a distillation during a commute (20-45 min)
2. Skimming the transcript to find a remembered quote
3. Comparing sections across documents
4. Reading in bed with dark mode

## Key Optimizations

### Typography
- Ensure text doesn't require zooming (min 16px effective)
- Optimal line length on narrow screens (45-65 characters)
- Proper paragraph spacing for visual breathing room
- Consider hyphenation for justified text (optional)

### Navigation Within Documents
- Sticky section headers that show current position
- Easy jump to section (table of contents)
- Progress indicator (already exists, verify it works well)
- 'Jump to top' accessible without scrolling up

### Visual Comfort
- Proper contrast ratios for outdoor/bright light reading
- Dark mode optimized for OLED screens (already have this)
- No harsh transitions when scrolling

### Interaction
- Smooth scrolling (verify momentum scrolling on iOS)
- Section anchors that work on mobile
- Easy copy/share of specific quotes

## Deliverables

- Typography refinements for mobile
- Document navigation improvements
- Reading-specific UI enhancements

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Text readable without zooming on 375px width
- [ ] Can navigate to any section within 2 taps
- [ ] Progress indicator visible during reading
- [ ] Dark mode doesn't cause eye strain
- [ ] Smooth scrolling on iOS and Android","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:26:32.540075774-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:13:23.972747175-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:13:23.972747175-05:00","close_reason":"Mobile reading experience complete: sticky section indicator, mobile typography optimizations (16px+ clamp), reading-width constraints (65ch), momentum scrolling for iOS, MobileTocFab with BottomSheet for quick navigation. All acceptance criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qka","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ldh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:23.760040476-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qka","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-3vw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:24.994109586-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-qtsb","title":"EPIC: The Complete Brenner Loop System","description":"# The Complete Brenner Loop System

## Vision Statement
Transform BrennerBot from a corpus browser into a full-featured **research methodology lab** that operationalizes the Brenner approach to discriminative hypothesis testing.

## Background & Rationale
The existing BrennerBot provides access to Brenner's primary source material (transcripts, distillations, quote banks) and explains the operators. But the real value of Brenner's methodology lies in **active application**:

1. **Formulating testable hypotheses** with falsifiable predictions
2. **Applying operators systematically** (Level Split, Exclusion Test, Object Transpose, Scale Check)
3. **Running discriminative tests** that could actually rule out possibilities
4. **Updating beliefs based on evidence** in a structured way
5. **Synthesizing insights** through multi-perspective analysis

This epic represents the complete transformation from 'documentation about methodology' to 'interactive methodology lab.'

## Core Insight
The Brenner approach is fundamentally about **preferring to be ruled out rather than ruled in**. A good hypothesis is one that makes bold predictions that could be wrong. The system should guide users toward thinking that way, not just explain the concepts.

## Success Criteria
- Users can run complete research sessions from hypothesis to conclusion
- The system guides users toward better (more discriminative) thinking
- Multi-agent synthesis provides genuine adversarial pressure
- All work products are durable artifacts with clear audit trails
- The experience is addictive because it genuinely improves thinking

## Key Dependencies
- Existing corpus infrastructure (complete)
- Tutorial system (in progress)
- Agent Mail integration (foundation exists)
- GA4 analytics (complete)

## Technical Constraints
- No vendor AI APIs in web app - coordinate via Agent Mail + external agents
- Must work gracefully when agents unavailable (degraded but functional)
- Must support offline-first for reading/reviewing (PWA future)

## Sub-Components (Each an Epic)
1. Hypothesis Engine - Core data models, cards, evolution tracking
2. Operator Application System - Interactive guided sessions for each operator
3. Multi-Agent Synthesis - Devil's Advocate, Experiment Designer, Brenner Channeler
4. Evidence Ledger - Test tracking, result recording, belief updates
5. Artifact Generation - Research Briefs, exports, structured outputs
6. Semantic Search & Intelligence - Vector search, confound detection
7. Session Experience & UI - Orchestration, state machines, progress
8. Infrastructure & API - Persistence, real-time, collaboration foundation

## Out of Scope for v1
- Multi-user real-time collaboration (deferred to v2)
- Public API (deferred to v2)
- Mobile native apps (web PWA sufficient for v1)

## Vision Statement
Transform BrennerBot from a corpus browser into a full-featured **research methodology lab** that operationalizes the Brenner approach to discriminative hypothesis testing.

## Background & Rationale
The existing BrennerBot provides access to Brenner's primary source material (transcripts, distillations, quote banks) and explains the operators. But the real value of Brenner's methodology lies in **active application**:

1. **Formulating testable hypotheses** with falsifiable predictions
2. **Applying operators systematically** (Level Split, Exclusion Test, Object Transpose, Scale Check)
3. **Running discriminative tests** that could actually rule out possibilities

This epic represents the complete transformation from 'documentation about methodology' to 'interactive methodology lab.'

## Core Insight
The Brenner approach is fundamentally about **preferring to be ruled out rather than ruled in**. A good hypothesis is one that makes bold predictions that could be wrong. The system should guide users toward thinking that way, not just explain the concepts.

## Success Criteria
- Users can run complete research sessions from hypothesis to conclusion
- The system guides users toward better (more discriminative) thinking

## Sub-Components (Each an Epic)
1. Hypothesis Engine - Core data models, cards, evolution tracking
2. Operator Application System - Interactive guided sessions for each operator
3. Multi-Agent Synthesis - Devil's Advocate, Experiment Designer, Brenner Channeler
4. Evidence Ledger - Test tracking, result recording, belief updates
5. Artifact Generation - Research Briefs, exports, structured outputs
6. Semantic Search & Intelligence - Vector search, confound detection
7. Session Experience & UI - Orchestration, state machines, progress
8. Infrastructure & API - Persistence, real-time, collaboration foundation

## Out of Scope for v1
- Multi-user real-time collaboration (deferred to v2)
- Public API (deferred to v2)
- Mobile native apps (web PWA sufficient for v1)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-qzr","title":"Web orchestration: Windows absolute projectKey should trigger ensure_project","description":"## Problem
`apps/web/src/app/api/sessions/route.ts` currently decides whether to call Agent Mail `ensure_project` using a POSIX-only check:

- `projectKey.startsWith("/")`

This skips `ensure_project` for Windows absolute paths (e.g. `C:\\repo` or `\\\\server\\share`), causing downstream Agent Mail calls to fail or behave inconsistently.

## Root cause
We’re conflating “absolute path” with “starts with /”.

## Fix
Use `isAbsolute(projectKey)` from `node:path` (cross-platform) for the decision.

## Acceptance
- Windows absolute project keys call `ensure_project`.
- Existing POSIX behavior unchanged.
- Web tests still pass (`cd apps/web && bun run test`).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PurplePond","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:03:36.022060056-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T08:07:00.770595997-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T08:07:00.770595997-05:00","close_reason":"Use node:path isAbsolute(projectKey) for ensure_project gating (Windows-safe); apps/web tests pass","labels":["agent-mail","orchestration","web","windows"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-qzr","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.3.2","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:03:36.048932296-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-r2c5","title":"Task: Compute Dashboard Metrics","description":"# Task: Compute Dashboard Metrics

## Objective
Implement computation of all dashboard metrics by aggregating data from hypotheses, tests, and sessions.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/program-metrics.ts`

### Core Function
```typescript
export async function computeProgramMetrics(
  programId: string
): Promise<ProgramMetrics> {
  const program = await loadProgram(programId);
  if (!program) throw new Error(`Program ${programId} not found`);
  
  // Load all data for this program
  const hypotheses = await loadProgramHypotheses(program.sessions);
  const tests = await loadProgramTests(program.sessions);
  const scores = await Promise.all(program.sessions.map(scoreSession));
  
  // Compute hypothesis metrics
  const byState = groupBy(hypotheses, h => h.state);
  const activeCount = byState.active?.length ?? 0;
  const killedCount = byState.killed?.length ?? 0;
  const validatedCount = byState.validated?.length ?? 0;
  const denominator = killedCount + validatedCount + activeCount;
  
  // Compute test metrics
  const completedTests = tests.filter(t => t.status === 'completed');
  const blockedTests = tests.filter(t => t.status === 'blocked');
  
  // Compute session metrics
  const avgScore = mean(scores.map(s => s.totalScore));
  const trend = calculateTrend(scores);
  
  // Compute time metrics
  const firstSession = minBy(scores, s => s.scoredAt);
  const lastSession = maxBy(scores, s => s.scoredAt);
  const daysActive = daysBetween(firstSession.scoredAt, lastSession.scoredAt);
  
  return {
    programId,
    totalHypotheses: hypotheses.length,
    activeHypotheses: activeCount,
    killedHypotheses: killedCount,
    validatedHypotheses: validatedCount,
    dormantHypotheses: byState.dormant?.length ?? 0,
    killRate: denominator > 0 ? killedCount / denominator : 0,
    totalTests: tests.length,
    completedTests: completedTests.length,
    blockedTests: blockedTests.length,
    testExecutionRate: tests.length > 0 ? completedTests.length / tests.length : 0,
    sessionCount: program.sessions.length,
    averageSessionScore: avgScore,
    scoreTrend: trend,
    daysActive,
    lastActivityAt: lastSession.scoredAt,
  };
}
```

### Helper Functions
```typescript
// Load all hypotheses across program sessions
async function loadProgramHypotheses(sessionIds: string[]): Promise<Hypothesis[]>

// Load all tests across program sessions
async function loadProgramTests(sessionIds: string[]): Promise<TestRecord[]>

// Calculate trend from chronological scores
function calculateTrend(scores: SessionScore[]): 'improving' | 'declining' | 'stable'

// Group items by key
function groupBy<T>(items: T[], keyFn: (item: T) => string): Record<string, T[]>
```

### Derived Metrics
```typescript
export interface ProgramHealth {
  status: 'healthy' | 'stalled' | 'blocked' | 'unknown';
  reasons: string[];
}

export function assessProgramHealth(metrics: ProgramMetrics): ProgramHealth {
  const reasons: string[] = [];
  
  // Check for stalled programs
  if (metrics.daysActive > 7 && metrics.killRate < 0.1) {
    reasons.push('Low kill rate - consider designing more discriminative tests');
  }
  
  // Check for blocked progress
  if (metrics.blockedTests > metrics.completedTests) {
    reasons.push('Many blocked tests - address blockers before proceeding');
  }
  
  // Check for declining trend
  if (metrics.scoreTrend === 'declining') {
    reasons.push('Session scores declining - review method adherence');
  }
  
  if (reasons.length === 0) return { status: 'healthy', reasons: [] };
  if (reasons.some(r => r.includes('blocked'))) return { status: 'blocked', reasons };
  return { status: 'stalled', reasons };
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All metrics computed from underlying data
- [ ] Hypothesis funnel metrics accurate
- [ ] Test execution rate calculated
- [ ] Session score trend computed
- [ ] Days active calculated correctly
- [ ] Health assessment provides actionable status

## Dependencies
- Task: Define Research Program Schema
- Task: Implement Program Storage Layer
- Feature: Hypothesis Lifecycle States
- Feature: Test→Hypothesis Binding
- Feature: Brenner Method Scorecard

## Effort Estimate
~200 lines of code, ~2.5 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:51:01.977985488-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T03:10:22.237897303-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T03:10:22.237897303-05:00","close_reason":"Dashboard CLI command and metrics computation implemented in 3a8834f","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-r2c5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-2qyl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:51:33.195834819-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-r2c5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-4buj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:51:33.38818684-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-r2c5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8b10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:51:33.419570168-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-r2c5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-6n4e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:51:33.450917879-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-r2c5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:51:33.482133-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-r2c5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:51:33.514855407-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-r2c5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:42:29.684732734-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
